mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate above the neutral level increases the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and raises debt-service costs, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor income and consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 45% of instances when UNRATE has risen more than 0.3 percentage points over the preceding 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in retail sales and increase in consumer prices generates a stagflationary shock that compresses corporate profit margins and reduces real household spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When RSAFS declines month-over-month while CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, above-trend industrial production signals an overheating economy that prompts additional Federal Reserve rate hikes, which reprices risk assets downward and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, elevated INDPRO fails to predict negative 20-day S&P 500 returns (Rank IC > 0.0 over a 2-year window).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets process the macro release.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth coincides with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of quarterly observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts signal accelerating construction activity and durable goods demand, which boosts cyclical corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate and rise in consumer prices indicate a tight labor market with accelerating inflation, which forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy faster than priced, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with month-over-month declines in UNRATE concurrent with month-over-month rises in CPIAUCSL fail to produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of cases over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month consumer price inflation exceeding 0.2 percent signals sticky inflation that reduces real earnings multiples and triggers risk-off repositioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months where CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.2%.
